title: daphne oleoides subsp. atlantica
intro: Daphne oleoides subsp. atlantica, also known as the Atlas laurel or Spurge laurel, is a shrub species that belongs to the Thymelaeaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Daphne oleoides subsp. atlantica include its ability to treat skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Daphne oleoides subsp. atlantica can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
